headlines.title: SpaceX aims to make its stock market debut at $1.77 trillion value
headlines.description: Elon Musk’s space company said in a securities filing that it hopes to sell over 555 million shares at its debut for $135 apiece.

headlines.title: Elon Musk is set to become the world’s first trillionaire. Democrats are taking aim.
headlines.description: Democrats are sharpening their attacks against Elon Musk, zeroing in on his soon-to-be-trillionaire status before his company SpaceX’s initial public offering.

headlines.title: Trump signs order seeking early access to powerful AI models before release
headlines.description: The president signed an order seeking to establish a voluntary framework for AI companies to provide the government early access to new models.

headlines.title: Around 1 in 5 young people use AI chatbots for mental health advice, survey finds
headlines.description: Nearly 1 in 5 adolescents and young adults are turning to AI chatbots for advice when they’re sad, angry, nervous or stressed, according to a new study.

headlines.title: Florida sues OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman, accusing them of putting profit over safety
headlines.description: Florida’s attorney general says the company misrepresents safety to turn a profit.

headlines.title: House Votes to Restrain Trump’s War Powers
headlines.description: There were cheers in the House of Representatives after four Republicans broke from President Donald Trump, joining Democrats to pass a preliminary resolution to stop the war on Iran. The president said a deal with Iran could come soon and that the near-daily exchanges of fire between the United States and Iran and attacks on U.S. allies on the Gulf, are normal. NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Trump to Tap Acting AG Todd Blanche to Lead DOJ Permanently
headlines.description: President Donald Trump is making changes at the Justice Department, nominating acting attorney general Todd Blanche as its permanent director. Blanche replaced Pam Bondi after her firing and since then has notably investigated several of Trump’s perceived enemies.

headlines.title: Marjane Satrapi, French-Iranian author of ‘Persepolis,’ dies at 56
headlines.description: Marjane Satrapi, the French-Iranian author and illustrator behind the popular graphic novel series and film “Persopolis,” has died, the French presidency said Thursday.

headlines.title: The Knicks’ Jalen Brunson can already do what the Spurs are still figuring out
headlines.description: When the New York Knicks needed a big basket against the San Antonio Spurs in Game 1 of the NBA Finals, they turned to Jalen Brunson. He delivered.

headlines.title: Telecommunications companies are adapting to natural disasters by investing in new tech
headlines.description: This hurricane season is expected to be relatively calm, but the U.S. could still see a major storm. NBC News' Gary Grumbach takes a look at how some telecommunications companies are utilizing new technology to better respond to natural disasters.

headlines.title: Florida Sues Open AI and Sam Altman Alleging Safety Issues
headlines.description: The state of Florida is suing OpenAI and its CEO Sam Altman, accusing the company of putting profit over safety, fueling violence and pushing a product it knew could harm users. The suit is separate from a criminal investigation into OpenAI that Florida’s attorney general opened in April.

headlines.title: TikTok documentary posted by Paris Hilton digs into notorious deepfake pornography website
headlines.description: Paris Hilton and independent journalist Laurie Segall fight the rise of AI-generated deepfakes in a TikTok documentary. It investigates the man believed to be behind a notorious deepfake pornography website. NBC News’ Chloe Melas reports.
